pi_items.source: Confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 13), export_result.sh (line 10), and task description (task.json line 5)
Task description explicitly states 'outer dimensions 60x40x30 mm'; confirmed in verifier comments (line 29) and verifier dimension checks (line 90)

Calculated from box geometry: Outer volume (60×40×30 = 72000 mm³) minus inner cavity (56×36×28 = 56448 mm³) equals 15552 mm³ material volume. Confirmed in verifier comment (line 29) and volume tolerance checks (lines 92-93)
Calculated from lid geometry: Main plate (60×40×4 = 9600 mm³) plus lip (56×36×3 = 6048 mm³) equals 15648 mm³. Confirmed in verifier comment (line 30) and volume tolerance checks (lines 108-109)

Used consistently in verifier.py (line 33) for tolerance calculation: TOLERANCE = metadata.get('volume_tolerance_percent', 10) / 100.0. Applied to both box and lid volume checks with ±10% tolerance window

privileged_info_summary: The task design_fitted_box_lid requires creating two part design bodies in FreeCAD: (1) a Box with outer dimensions 60×40×30 mm and 2mm uniform wall thickness (open top) containing 15,552 mm³ of material, and (2) a Lid consisting of a 60×40×4 mm main plate plus a 56×36×3 mm alignment lip totaling 15,648 mm³. The output file must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/project_box_assembly.FCStd. All volume targets are verified mathematically correct with a ±10% tolerance margin for verification.
